## [03:05 - 06:07]

your incremental 20% increases. This is just an example of a campaign which I ran where around 20% increases, and I was validating that the ad set, which was on $500 a day, was able to take just small incremental increases and spend and within a short period of time, with more than double the budget. So, you know, it's about the compound effect of 20% increase as well, and being able to validate whether the ad sets ready. The next part of this is then optimizing your ads. So if you've done the V scaling, at the same time, you'll probably find ads that are maybe not working as well as the other ads. So my best practice is running three ads per ad set, potentially four or five ads if I've got the budget to support it, or I want to test a few more variations up my ads. And as you start to scale up, you may find that certain ads are not performing as well. And that's where the ad sniper strategy comes in. So have a look at the ad sniper strategy if you haven't, to optimize your ads and pull out the bad performers. And so what I'm doing here is to find the ads which are not performing as well as maybe some of the other ads, pausing them and moving on. And this works at the ad set level, where I'll be looking at the average results across the ad set, and then trimming ads which are not up to scratch versus the ad set average. And then the kind of fourth part when it comes to scaling, and especially if I want to scale faster, so in some cases 20% incremental increase and spend a day every few days is fine. but it scales a lot faster. So if you imagine I had this ad set at $283.5, and that's because of the 20% scaling previously, I've not touched that. So I've less that running as it was.
